title: Audit Editorial Content v1.0
$comment: Prose overrides only. This schema carries human-written content that overrides generated text in the audit and remediation files. It does not carry remediation structure. Joined to audit and remediation files by audit_id. The front-end merges this with the data JSON at render time, preferring editorial content when present. All sections are optional; the front-end falls back to data JSON content when editorial content is absent.

schema_version.$comment: Editorial schema version. Independent from audit schema version.

audit_ref.$comment: Must match the audit_id in the companion data JSON. Links the two files.

last_edited_by.$comment: Optional. Name of the last person who edited this file.

last_edited_at.$comment: Optional. ISO 8601 timestamp of the last edit.

report.$comment: Optional. Report-level prose for client-facing output. Has no counterpart in the data JSON.

title.$comment: Client-facing report title, e.g. 'AI-Readiness Audit: Material UI'.

executive_summary.$comment: Overall narrative for stakeholders. Two to four sentences summarising the audit state and the single most important action.

methodology_note.$comment: Brief explanation of the audit approach for readers unfamiliar with the methodology.

clusters.$comment: Optional. Keyed by cluster key (e.g. '1_token_and_variable_system'). Overrides or supplements cluster-level prose in the data JSON.

narrative.$comment: Editorial override of the cluster_summary field in the data JSON. Used in dashboards and executive summaries.

value_framing.$comment: What this cluster's current state means operationally. Used on the Impact screen. Example: 'Token architecture gaps mean every theme change requires manual updates across all components using flat values.'

dimensions.$comment: Optional. Keyed by dimension ID (e.g. '3.1_component_description_coverage'). Overrides dimension-level prose in the data JSON.

narrative.$comment: Editorial override of the dimension narrative field in the data JSON. Used in dimension detail views.

findings.$comment: Optional. Keyed by finding ID (e.g. 'CDC-001'). Overrides finding-level prose in the data JSON. The front-end prefers these values when present.

summary.$comment: Edited one-line overview. Overrides the summary field in the data JSON.

description.$comment: Edited full description. Overrides the description field in the data JSON.

recommendation.$comment: Edited recommendation text. Overrides the recommendation field in the data JSON.

remediation.$comment: Optional. Keyed by remediation item ID (e.g. 'REM-001'). Overrides remediation-level prose in the data JSON.

action.$comment: Edited action text. Overrides the action field in the data JSON.

value_framing.$comment: Edited operational consequence text. Overrides the value_framing field in the data JSON.
